ananda.name: Ānanda
ananda.title: The Beloved Attendant
ananda.bio: The Buddha's cousin and personal attendant for 25 years. Known for memorizing every discourse the Buddha gave. Embodiment of devoted service and unbroken attention.
ananda.abilityDesc: Ānanda remembered every teaching the Buddha gave. As a player with this gift, when your quest advances a stage, you collect two wisdom scrolls instead of one — the normal reward plus an extra teaching on the same theme.

sariputta.name: Sāriputta
sariputta.title: General of the Dhamma
sariputta.bio: The Buddha's chief disciple in wisdom. Could analyze any teaching to its root. Foremost in understanding the deepest points of dhamma.
sariputta.abilityDesc: Sāriputta turned every setback into understanding. Once per week, if you miss a key habit, you can write a short journal entry about what blocked you — and the Shadow does not grow from that miss. The loss becomes a teaching.
sariputta.abilityEffect: 1×/week: journal a miss instead of Shadow damage

moggallana.name: Mahā Moggallāna
moggallana.title: Master of Powers
moggallana.bio: Sāriputta's closest friend and the Buddha's chief disciple in psychic power. Could see across realms and reach those in trouble.
moggallana.abilityDesc: Moggallāna could reach anyone in trouble, anywhere. When a teammate is struggling and their personal Shadow is rising, you can transfer 10 of your own daily points to them as a rescue — reducing their Shadow and keeping their streak alive.
moggallana.abilityEffect: Gift 10 pts to a teammate to reduce their Shadow

mahakassapa.name: Mahā Kassapa
mahakassapa.title: Foremost in Ascetic Practice
mahakassapa.bio: The most rigorous of the disciples. Embodied the dhutaṅga practices — austerity, simplicity, contentment with little. Led the First Council after the Buddha's death.
mahakassapa.abilityDesc: Kassapa was immovable in discipline. As a player with this gift, the Shadow has a harder time growing against you — your total Shadow level is reduced by 3 points at all times, making Mara slower to reach the danger zone.

anuruddha.name: Anuruddha
anuruddha.title: Divine Eye
anuruddha.bio: The Buddha's cousin. Foremost in clairvoyance. Famous for once falling asleep during a teaching, vowing never to sleep again, and going blind from the effort — then developing the divine eye instead.
anuruddha.abilityDesc: Anuruddha could see far ahead with his inner eye. As a player with this gift, you can preview the next stage's Māra armies before they arrive, so you know what challenges are coming and can prepare.
anuruddha.abilityEffect: Preview next stage's armies in advance

upali.name: Upāli
upali.title: Master of Discipline
upali.bio: Born a barber, became foremost in Vinaya — the monastic discipline. Memorized the entire code of training rules. Proof that the path is open to anyone willing to walk it.
upali.abilityDesc: Upāli held the training rules with perfect steadiness. As a player with this gift, one missed day per week does not break your habit streaks — the streak continues as if the missed day never happened.

mahapajapati.name: Mahāpajāpatī
mahapajapati.title: First of the Bhikkhunīs
mahapajapati.bio: The Buddha's aunt and foster mother. The first woman ordained, founder of the bhikkhunī sangha. Walked from Kapilavatthu to Vesāli with shaved head and bare feet to make the path open to women.
mahapajapati.abilityDesc: Mahāpajāpatī held the entire sangha up when times were hard. As a player with this gift, when the team Shadow rises above 60% (the danger zone), every teammate gains +20% points from all habits — helping the sangha recover together.
mahapajapati.abilityEffect: When team Shadow >60%: all teammates +20% points

khema.name: Khemā
khema.title: Foremost in Wisdom
khema.bio: A queen of remarkable beauty who once feared meeting the Buddha because she clung to her appearance. After meeting him, she awakened. Became the foremost in wisdom among the bhikkhunīs.
khema.abilityDesc: Khemā saw through Māra's deceptions with pure wisdom. As a player with this gift, once per stage you can instantly dismiss one Māra army challenge without having to complete its 3-day test — your seeing alone defeats it.
